name: .introduction
vignettes:
HERE IS COMMENT FOR THIS DOCUMENT [critical] is the level for options. 0: options are printed when call option --check; 1: options are printed when call crace (default); 2: implemented but not perfect; 3: should be implemented but not finished; 4: may be used in the future; 5: never used in crace any more; 6: never printed for users; 7: for devolopers. 

[format] in this file : \u001b[<code>m [1mbold[0m: 1 [3italic[0m: 3[4munderline[0m: 4 ]8;;https://www.url.com\url]8;;\: '\u001b]8;;url\u001b\[name]\u001b]8;;\u001b\\'

name: .help
description: Show the information of crace options.
vignettes: Show the list of command-line options of crace. 1. brief information when call with [1m-h[0m and2. detail information when call with [1m--help[0m. 3. more details when call [1m--man [option][0m.

name: .man
description: Show details of the selected option.
vignettes: Show details of the selected option. call with [1mpython crace --man [option][0m

name: .version
description: Show crace package version.
vignettes: Show the version of crace. 1. brief information when call with [1m-v[0m and 2. detail information when call with [1m--version[0m.

name: .check
description: Check the provided scenario files.
vignettes: Check if the scenario and parameter definitions are correct and test the execution of the target algorithm [1m3[0m times on one training instance and one test instance (if provided), respectively. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=11\<User Guide: sec:step>]8;;\ for more details.

name: .onlytest
description: Only test the configurations given in the file passed as argument.
vignettes: Run the configurations contained in the file provided (with option [4mconfigurationsFile[0m) as argument on the test instances. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=32\<User Guide: sec:testing>]8;;\ for more details.

name: .initialize
description: Initialize the tuning directory with template scenario files.
vignettes: Initialize the tuning directory with template scenario files. With this option, two scenarios and some script files are copied to current folder.

name: .readlogs
description: Read data from log files of previous run.
vignettes: Read data from log files of previous run and return a craceReaults object, which includes objects CraceOptions, Parameters, Configurations, Instances, Models and Experiments.

name: readLogsInCplot
description: Read data from log files of previous run in plot or not.
vignettes: Read data from log files of previous run in plot or not.

name: seed
description: Seed of the random number for generator (by default, generate a random seed).
vignettes: Seed to initiallize the random number generator. The seed must be a positive integer. If the seed is ""or NULL, a random seed will be generated.

name: debugLevel
description: Debug level of the output of crace. Set this to 0 to silence all debug messages. Higher values provide more verbose debug messages.
vignettes: Level of information to display in the text output of crace. A value of 0 silences all debug messages. Higher values provide more verbose debug messages. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=36\<User Guide: sec:output text>]8;;\ for more details.

name: logLevel
description: Level to generate log files. When it is 0: only critical files for recovery and plotting will be generated.
vignettes: Level to generate log files. 0: only necessary files for the recovering and plots are included. 1: plus execution_log and race_log files. 2: plus race file. 4: plus mpi log files. 5: all files.

name: execDir
description: Directory where the programs will be run.
vignettes: Directory where the target algorithm executions will be performed. The default execution directory is the current directory. [1mNotice[0m: The execution directory must exist before executing crace, it will not be created automatically.

name: logDir
description: Directory to save tuning results as plain text files, either absolute path or relative to execDir.
vignettes: Directory to save tuning results as plain text files. The provided path must be either an absolute path or relative to option [4mexecDir[0m. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=36\<User Guide: sec:logs>]8;;\ for details on the format of the plain text files.

name: recoveryDir
description: Previously saved log files to recover the execution of crace, either absolute path or relative to the current directory. If empty or NULL, recovery is not performed.
vignettes: Previously saved crace log files that should be used to recover the execution of crace; either absolute path or relative to the current directory. If empty or NULL, recovery is not performed. For more details about recovery. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=33\<User Guide: sec:recovery>]8;;\ for more details.

name: scenarioFile
description: File that describes the configuration scenario setup and other crace settings.
vignettes: File that contains the scenario setup and other crace options. All options listed in this section can be included in this file. Relative file-system paths specified in the scenario file are relative to the scenario file itself.See $CRACEHOME/inst/templates/ for an example.
man: Path of the file that describes the configuration scenario setup and other crace settings.

name: parameterFile
description: File that contains the description of the parameters of the target algorithm.
vignettes: File that contains the description of the parameters of the target algorithm. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=16\<User Guide: sec:parameters file>]8;;\ for more details.

name: forbiddenFile
description: File that contains a list of logical expressions that cannot be TRUE for any evaluated configurations. If empty or NULL, do not use forbidden expressions.
vignettes: File containing a list of logical expressions that cannot be true for any evaluated configuration. If empty or NULL, no forbidden configurations are considered. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=15\<User Guide: sec:forbidden>]8;;\ for more details.

name: configurationsFile
description: File that contains a table of initial configurations. If empty or NULL, all initial configurations are randomly generated.
vignettes: File containing a table of initial configurations. If empty or NULL, all initial configurations will be sampled ramdonly. The provided configurations must not violate the constraints described in option [4mparameterFile[0m and [4mparameterforbiddenFile[0m.See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=23\<User Guide: sec:initial>]8;;\ for more details.

name: trainInstancesDir
description: Directory where training instances are located; either absolute path or relative to current directory. If no trainInstancesFiles is provided, all the files in trainInstancesDir will be listed as instances.
vignettes: Directory where training instances are located; either absolute path or relative to current directory. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf\<User Guide: sec:training>]8;;\ for more details.

name: trainInstancesFile
description: File that contains a list of training instances and optionally additional parameters for them. If trainInstancesDir is provided, crace will search for the files in this folder.
vignettes: File that contains a list of instances and optionally additional parameters for them.The list of instances in option [4mtrainInstancesFile[0m is interpreted as file-system paths relative to option [4mtrainInstancesDir[0m. When using an absolute path or instances that are not files, set option [4mtrainInstancesDir=""[0m. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=20\<User Guide: sec:training>]8;;\ for more details.

name: sampleInstances
description: Randomly sample the training instances or use them in the order given.
vignettes: Enable/disable the sampling of the training instances. If the option [4msampleInstances[0m is disabled, the instances are used in the order provided in the [4mtrainInstancesFile[0m or in the order they are read from the [4mtrainInstancesDir[0m when [4mtrainInstancesFile[0m is not provided. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=23\<User Guide: sec:training>]8;;\ for more details.

name: testInstancesDir
description: Directory where testing instances are located, either absolute or relative to current directory. Instances provided for testing should be different from the training instances.
vignettes: Directory where testing instances are located, either absolute or relative to the current directory. Instances provided for testing should be different from the training instances. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=31\<User Guide: sec:testing>]8;;\

name: testInstancesFile
description: File that contains a list of test instances and optionally additional parameters for them. Instances provided for testing should be different from the training instances.
vignettes: File that contains a list of test instances and optionally additional parameters for them. The list of instances in option [4mtestInstancesFile[0m is interpreted as file-system paths relative to option [4mtestInstancesDir[0m. When using an absolute path or instances that are not files, set option [4mtestInstancesDir=""[0m. Instances provided for testing should be different from the training instances.See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=31\<User Guide: sec:tesing>]8;;\ for more details.

name: testNbElites
description: Number of elite configurations returned by crace that will be tested if test instances are provided.
vignettes: Number of elite configurations returned by crace that will be tested if test instances are provided. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=32\<User Guide: sec:tesing>]8;;\ for more details.

name: testType
domain: (f-test, ftest, friedman, t-test, ttest, t-test-bonferroni, t-test-bon, t-test-holm)
description: Statistical test used for elimination. Default test is always [1mF-test[0m unless capping is enabled, in which case the default test is adaptive-dom.
vignettes:
Specifies the statistical test used for elimination: 
F-test: Friedman test. 
t-test: pairwise t-tests with no correction. 
t-test-bonferroni: t-test with Bonferroni's correction for multiple comparisons. 
t-test-holm: t-test with Holm's correction for multiple comparisons. 

We recommend to not use corrections for multiple comparisons because the test typically becomes too strict and the search stagnates. The default setting of parameter testType is F-test unless the option [4mcapping[0m is enabled in which case, the default setting is defined as adaptive-dom. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=27\<User Guide: sec:stat test>]8;;\ for more details about choosing the statistical test most appropriate for your scenario.

name: domType
description: Dominance test used for elimination. Default test is always [1madaptive-dom[0m when capping is enabled. Dominance is for capping and adaptive dominance is for adaptive capping.
vignettes: Specifies the dominance test used for elimination:dominance test is derived from capping, adaptive dominance test is derived from adaptive capping.

name: eachTest
description: Number of instances evaluated for each configuration between elimination tests.
vignettes: Specifies how many instances are evaluated for each configuration between elimination tests.

name: firstTest
description: Number of instances evaluated before the first elimination test. It must be a multiple of [1meachTest[0m.
vignettes: Specifies how many instances are evaluated before the first elimination test. The value of option [4mfirstTest[0m must be a multiple of the value of option [4meachTest[0m. When option [4mcapping[0m is enabled, the default value is [1m1[0m; while option [4mcapping[0m is disabled, the default value is [1m5[0m.

name: testExperimentSize
description: Number of finished target executions should be included in one elimination test.
vignettes: Specifies the number of finished target executions should be included in one elimination test. This option can decrease the frequency to call elimination test.The values [1m0[0m or [1m1[0m mean to call elimination test when one target execution is finished. The greater number, the lower frequency to call test.

name: confidence
description: Confidence level for the elimination test.
vignettes: Confidence level for the elimination test.

name: eliteRef
description: Number of elite configurations that should have a better performance when removing a configuration.
vignettes: Specifies how many configurations should have a better performance when removing a configuration.

name: targetRunner
description: Script called for each configuration that executes the target algorithm to be tuned. See templates.
vignettes: Define a script that evaluates a configuration of the target algorithm on a particular instance. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=19\<User Guide: sec:runner>]8;;\ for more details.

name: targetRunnerLauncher
description: An executable command line called for each configuration that executes the target algorithm to be tuned. See templates.
vignettes: An executable command line that call a script file using specific executable software for each configuration that executes the target algorithm on a particular instance, when [4mtargetRunner[0m cannot be executed directly. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=19\<User Guide: sec:runner>]8;;\ for more details.

name: targetRunnerRetries
description: Number of times to retry a call to the target algorithm if the call failed.
vignettes: Number of times to retry a call to the target algorithm (provided by option [4mtargetRunner[0m) if the call failed.

name: maxExperiments
description: Maximum number of runs (invocations of the targetRunner) that will be performed. It determines the maximum budget of experiments for the tuning.
vignettes: The maximum number of runs (invocations of the target algorithm that is provided by option [4mtargetRunner[0m) that will be performed. It determines the maximum budget of experiments for the tuning. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=26\<User Guide: sec:budget>]8;;\ for more details.

name: maxTime
description: Maximum total execution time in seconds for the executions of targetRunner that must return two values: cost and time.
vignettes: The maximum total time in seconds for the runs of targetRunner that will be performed.When option [4mmaxTime[0m is positive, the [4mtargetRunner[0m [1mmust[0m return the execution time as its second output. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=26\<User Guide: sec:budget>]8;;\ for more details.

name: digits
description: Maximum number of decimal places that are significant for numerical (real) parameters.
vignettes: Maximum number of decimal places that are significant for numerical (real) parameters.

name: deterministic
description: If the target algorithm is deterministic, configurations will be evaluated only once per instance.
vignettes: Enable/disable deterministic target algorithm mode. If the target algorithm is deterministic, configurations will be evaluated only once per instance. If the number of instances provided is less than the value specified for the option [4mfirstTest[0m, no statistical test will be performed. If instances provided are all used, crace will be terminated.See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf\<User Guide: sec:training>]8;;\ for more details.

name: parallel
description: Number of calls to execute targetRunner in parallel. Values 0 or 1 mean no parallelization.
vignettes: Number of calls of the [4mtargetRunner[0m to be executed in parallel. Values 0 or 1 mean no parallelization. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=31\<User Guide: sec:parallel>]8;;\ for more details.

name: mpi
description: Enable/disable MPI. Use mpi to execute targetRunner in parallel (the number of slaves).
vignettes: Enable/disable the use of package [1mmpi4py[0m to execute the targetRunner in parallel using MPI protocol.When option [4mmpi[0m is enabled, the option [4mparallel[0m is the number of slave nodes. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=31\<User Guide: sec:parallel>]8;;\ for more details.

name: bindcores
description: Enable/disable binding cores to physical node. Use bindcores to bind each worker to specific node.
vignettes: Enable/disable the bindings of workers to correspoding cores when using paralel settings, especially on system allowed mpi tools. ONLY supported on linux.

name: nbConfigurations
description: Minimum number of alive configurations in the racing procedure.
vignettes: The minimum number of alive configurations that will be tuned in the racing procedure. By default (when equal to 0), this value will be determined by the number of parameters of the provided scenario.

name: maxNbElites
description: Maximum number of elite configurations selected in the racing procedure.
vignettes: Maximum number of elite configurations selected in the racing procedure. By default (when equal to 0), this value will be set as 6.

name: elitist
description: Enable/disable elitist crace. Enable means always to select the configurations with the most executions as elites.
vignettes: Enable/disable elitist crace.In the [1melitist[0m version of crace, only configurations having the most executions can be selected as elites, which are used for sampling new configurations or updating the models.In the elite version of crace, at most [4mmaxNbElites[0m configurations in the top of rank from elimination test will be selected as elites.

name: globalModel
description: Enable/disable global model. Enable means always to select the global model to sample new configurations and update models.
vignettes: Enable/disable global model.In the [1melitist[0m version of crace, only global model is alive during the race procedure, which is used for sampling new configurations or updating the model.It can only be activated when [4mmelitist[0m is enable.

name: modelUpdateByStep
vignettes: Number of times to update the model(s) in the race. The default number is based on the number of parameters of the provided scenario. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=35\<User Guide: sec:output text>]8;;\ for more details.
description: Number of times to update the model(s) in the race

name: sampleInsStrict
description: Sample instance immediately or not when there is a challenge.
vignettes: Enable/disable the sampling of the training instances strictly. If the option [4msampleInsStrict[0m is disabled, the challenge is discovered when there is no enough alive configuration. Otherwise, the challenge is discovered immediately.

name: sampleInsFrequency
description: The probability for sampling new instance when there is a challenge.
vignettes: The probability for sampling new instance when there is a challenge. The default value is 1, which means it will always sample a new instance when there is a challenge, which means there are statistical similar configurations when using statistical test and there is a new elite configuration wins the old with the most instances when using aggresive test.

name: instancePatience
description: The number of configurations that an instance can undergo without yielding any improvement.
vignettes: The number of configurations that an instance can undergo without yielding any improvement. The default value is 0, which means there is no such a limitation for configurations.

name: softRestart
description: The number of times of the soft restart strategy applied to avoid premature convergence of the probabilistic model.
vignettes: The number of times of the soft-restart strategy applied to avoid premature convergence of the probabilistic model. When there is no new configuration after 100 tries, the probabilistic model of alive configurations is soft restarted. The default value is [1m-1[0m, indicating an unlimited number of restart attempts. and the racing procedure will be terminated when all budgets are exhausted.

name: capping
description: Enable/disable the use of capping, a technique designed for minimizing the computation time of configurations. This is only available when elitist is active.
vignettes: Enable/disable the use of capping, a technique designed for minimizing the computation time of configurations. This option is only available when option [4melitist[0m is activated. When enabling this option, crace provides an execution time bound to each target algorithm execution (see ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=28\<User Guide: sec:aggres test>]8;;\). The bound can be calculated using capping or adaptive capping. See ]8;;https://race-autoconfig.github.io/crace/crace-package.pdf#page=29\<User Guide: sec:capping>]8;;\ for more details.

name: boundMax
description: Maximum execution time bound for targetRunner. It must be specified when capping is enabled.
vignettes: Maximum execution time bound for each target algorithm execution when option [4mcapping[0m is enabled.

name: boundMaxDEV
description: Maximum execution bound for [4mtargetRunner[0m especially for training phase. It could be specified when capping is enabled.
vignettes: Maximum execution bound for [4mtargetRunner[0m especially for training phase. It could be specified when capping is enabled and you wish to use a different runtime bound from the test phase.

name: boundDigits
description: Precision used for calculating the execution time. It must be specified when capping is enabled.
vignettes: Precision used for calculating the execution time. It must be specified when capping is enabled.

name: boundAsTimeout
description: Replace the configuration cost of bounded executions with boundMax. It must be specified when capping is enabled
vignettes: Replace the configuration cost of bounded executions with the value of option [4mboundMax[0m. It must be specified when capping is enabled.

name: shuffleInstances
description: Randomly shuffle the training instances during the race.
vignettes: Enable/disable the shuffling of the training instances. If the option \parameter{shuffleInstances} is disabled, the instances are executed in the provided or \sampled order (see parameter{sampleInstances}).

name: testIterationElites
description: Enable/disable testing the elite configurations found at each iteration.
vignettes: Enable/disable testing the elite configurations found at each iteration.

name: targetRunnerParallel
description: Optional R function to provide custom parallelization of \code{targetRunner}.
vignettes: Optional \aR function to provide custom parallelization of \parameter{targetRunner}. See \autoref{sec:parallel} for more information.

name: targetRunnerData
description: Optional data passed to \code{targetRunner}. This is ignored by the default \code{targetRunner} function, but it may be used by custom \code{targetRunner} functions to pass persistent data around.
vignettes: Optional data passed to \parameter{targetRunner}. This is ignored by the default \parameter{targetRunner} function, but it may be used by custom \parameter{targetRunner} functions to pass persistent data around.

name: targetEvaluator
description: Optional script or R function that provides a numeric value for each configuration. See templates/target-evaluator.tmpl
vignettes: Optional script or \aR function that returns a numerical value for an experiment after all configurations have been executed on a given instance using \parameter{targetRunner}. See \autoref{sec:evaluator} for details.

name: initConfigurations
man: Data frame describing initial configurations (usually read from a file using \code{readConfigurations}).

name: repairConfiguration
description: User-defined R function that takes a configuration generated by crace and repairs it.
vignettes: User-defined \aR function that takes a configuration generated by crace and repairs it. See \autoref{sec:repairconf} for details.

name: forbiddenExps
man: Vector of R logical expressions that cannot evaluate to \code{TRUE} for any evaluated configuration.

name: testInstances
man: Character vector of the instances to be used in the \code{targetRunner} when executing the testing.

name: instances
man: Character vector of the instances to be used in the \code{targetRunner}.

name: eliteInstances
description: Percentage of the instances in the instance stream required to be executed on a candidate configuration in order to be considered candidate elite.
vignettes: Percentage of the instances in the instance stream required to be executed on a candidate configuration in order to be considered candidate elite. By default the value is 0.75, that is, 75% of the instances in the stream.

name: expectInstances
description: Expect minimal percentage of the provided training instances to be used during the whole training process.
vignettes: The minimal percentage of the provided training instances in the training process required to be used in total. By default the value is 0, that is, the number of used instances is decided by crace itself. The number of expected instances will be adjusted to never be lower than the value of \parameter{firstTest}. Instance is sampled based on this rule after finishing 25% budget.

name: elitistNewInstances
description: Number of instances added to the execution list before previous instances in elitist crace.
vignettes: Number of new instances added to each race before evaluating instances from previous races (only for elitist crace).\begin{xwarningbox}If \parameter{deterministic} is \code{TRUE} then the number of \parameter{elitistNewInstances} will be reduced or set to \code{0} once all instances have been evaluated. \end{xwarningbox}

name: elitistLimit
description: In elitist crace, maximum number per race of elimination tests that do not eliminate a configuration. Use 0 for no limit.
vignettes: Maximum number of statistical tests performed without successful elimination after all instances from the previous race have been evaluated. If the limit is reached, the current race is stopped. Only valid for elitist crace. Use \code{0} to disable the limit.

name: nbIterations
description: Number of iterations.
vignettes: Minimum number of iterations to be executed. Each iteration involves the generation of new configurations and the use of racing to select the best configurations. By default (with 0), crace calculates the minimum number of iterations as $\Niter = \lfloor 2 + \log_{2}\Nparam \rfloor$, where $\Nparam$ is the number of non-fixed parameters to be tuned. We recommend to use the default value.

name: nbExperimentsPerIteration
description: Number of runs of the target algorithm per iteration.
vignettes: Number of runs of the target algorithm per iteration. By default (when equal to 0), this value changes for each iteration and depends on the iteration index and the remaining budget. Further details are provided in the \irace paper~\citep{LopDubPerStuBir2016irace}.We recommend to use the default value.

name: mu
vignettes: Parameter used to define the number of configurations to be sampled and evaluated at each iteration. The number of configurations will be calculated such that there is enough budget in each race to evaluate all configurations on at least $\mu + \min(5,j)$ training instances, where $j$ is the index of the current iteration. The value of $\mu$ will be adjusted to never be lower than the value of \parameter{firstTest}. We recommend to use the default value and, if needed, adjust \parameter{firstTest}and \parameter{eachTest}, instead.
description: Parameter used to define the number of configurations sampled and evaluated at each iteration.

name: budgetEstimation
description: Fraction (smaller than 1) of the budget used to estimate the mean computation time of a configuration. Only used when \code{maxTime} > 0
vignettes: Fraction (smaller than 1) of the budget used to estimate the mean execution time of a configuration. Only used when \parameter{maxTime} $> 0$. See \autoref{sec:budget}.

name: boundPar
vignettes: Penalty used for PARX. This value is used to penalize timed out executions, see \autoref{sec:capping}.
description: Penalization constant for timed out executions (executions that reach \code{boundMax} execution time).

name: postselection
description: Percentage of the configuration budget used to perform a postselection race of the best configurations of each iteration after the execution of crace.
vignettes: Percentage of the configuration budget used to perform a postselection race of the best configurations of each iteration after the execution of crace. See \autoref{sec:postselection}.

name: minMeasurableTime
description: Minimum time unit that is still (significantly) measureable.
vignettes: Minimum time unit that is still (significantly) measureable.

name: softRestartThreshold
description: Soft restart threshold value for numerical parameters. If \code{NA}, NULL or \code{""}, it is computed as \code{10^-digits}.
vignettes: Soft restart threshold value for numerical parameters. By default, it is computed as $10^{-digits}$, where \parameter{digits} corresponds to the crace option explained in this section.

name: shuffleInstancesOnNew
description: Randomly shuffle the training instances during the race when adding a new instance.
vignettes: Enable/disable the shuffling of the training instances when adding a new instance. If the option \parameter{shuffleInstances} is disabled, the instances are executed in the provided or \sampled order (see parameter{sampleInstances}).

name: shuffleInstancesOnTest
description: Randomly shuffle the training instances during the race when a n tests cannot eliminate a configuration.
vignettes: Enable/disable the shuffling of the training instances when a n tests cannot eliminate a configuration. If the option \parameter{shuffleInstances} is disabled, the instances are executed in the provided or \sampled order (see parameter{sampleInstances}).

name: loadBalancing
description: Enable/disable load-balancing when executing experiments in parallel. Load-balancing makes better use of computing resources, but increases communication overhead. If this overhead is large, disabling load-balancing may be faster.
vignettes: Enable/disable load-balancing when executing experiments in parallel. Load-balancing makes better use of computing resources, but increases communication overhead. If this overhead is large, disabling load-balancing may be faster. See \autoref{sec:parallel}.

name: batchmode
description: Specify how crace waits for jobs to finish when \code{targetRunner} submits jobs to a batch cluster: sge, pbs, torque or slurm. \code{targetRunner} must submit jobs to the cluster using, for example, \code{qsub}.
vignettes: Specify how crace waits for jobs to finish when \parameter{targetRunner} submits jobs to a batch cluster: \code{sge}, \code{pbs}, \code{torque} or \code{slurm} (\parameter{targetRunner} must submit jobs to the cluster using. for example, \code{qsub}). See \autoref{sec:parallel}.

name: aclib
description: Enable/disable AClib mode. This option enables compatibility with GenericWrapper4AC as targetRunner script.
vignettes: Enable/disable AClib mode. This option enables compatibility with \texttt{GenericWrapper4AC} (\url{https://github.com/automl/GenericWrapper4AC/}) as \parameter{targetRunner} script.

name: cappingType
description: Measure used to obtain the execution bound from the performance of the elite configurations: median, mean, worst, best.
vignettes: Specifies the measure used to define the execution bound: \begin{itemize} \item[] \code{median} (the median of the performance of the elite configurations) \item[] \code{mean} (the mean of the performance of the elite configurations) \item[] \code{best} (the best performance of the elite configurations) \item[] \code{worst} (the worst performance of the elite configurations). \end{itemize}
man: Measure used to obtain the execution bound from the performance of the elite configurations.\itemize{\item median: Median performance of the elite configurations.\item mean: Mean performance of the elite configurations.\item best: Best performance of the elite configurations.\item worst: Worst performance of the elite configurations.}

name: model
description: Type of sampling model to be used [local, global, dglobal, mixed, dmixed].
vignettes: Selection of the type of sampling model to be used in crace.

name: pmodel
description: Percentage of the configurations generated with the local model, only used when the option model is set to mixed or dmixed.
vignettes: Percentage of the configurations sampled with the local model, only used when \parameter{model} is set to \texttt{mixed} or \texttt{dmixed}

name: imodel
description: Use incremental dependencies when using dependent model, only used when the option model is set to dglobal or dmixed.
vignettes: Use incremental dependencies when using dependent model, only used when \parameter{model} is set to \texttt{dglobal} or \texttt{dmixed}

name: itmodel
description: Number of iterations (max) to obtain the data required to train the dependent global model, only used when model is set to dglobal or dmixed.
vignettes: Number of iterations (max)to obtain the data required to train the dependent global model,  only used when \parameter{model} is set to \texttt{dglobal} or \texttt{dmixed}
